calendar.matters:
Petitioners The Estate Of Troy D. Rutman, Keith Rutman, Todd Rutman And Seth Rutman'S Petition Of Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jan-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Petitioner Keith Rutman telephone number 619-237-9072 in Propria Persona.
Wayne Snodgrass of Office of the City Attorney telephone number 415-554-4675 for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

Mr. Snodgrass stated that he was not contesting the tentative ruling, but that he had an issue with respect to language in the proposed order regarding attorney fees and a briefing schedule that is not contained in the tentative. Petitioners The Estate Of Troy D. Rutman, Keith Rutman, Todd Rutman and Seth Rutman's Petition for Writ Of Mandate is granted. The court orders Respondents to provide Petitioners with the documents which Petitioners requested in their California Public Records Act requests. Government Code section 6254(f) exempts disclosure of documents requested under the California Public Records Act if the disclosure would endanger the safety of a witness or other person involved in the investigation, or if disclosure would endanger the successful completion of the investigation or a related investigation. (Government Code section 6254(f).) Furthermore, the exemption applies only when there is "a concrete and definite prospect" of "criminal law enforcement" proceedings. (Dixon v. Superior Court (2009) 170 Cal.App.4th 1271, 1277.) The exemption does not apply to the documents Petitioners have requested. The evidence which Respondents present to support the position that disclosure of the documents would endanger the criminal investigation is insubstantial and weak. The investigation has been pending for over sixteen months, Respondent admits that Troy Rutman's death is no longer being actively investigated, and the District Attorney's Office has declined to file charges. The declaration statements of Sergeant Cagney that investigations typically last two years and that there is a reasonable possibility that evidence will come to light in the future do not establish that there is "a concrete and definite prospect" of "criminal law enforcement" proceedings or that there is an ongoing investigation that would be endangered if the documents were released. (Cagney Decl paragraph 2, 7-9, 11.) Although Petitioners are entitled to attorney's fees under Government Code section 6259, the court is unable to award attorney's fees at this time because Petitioners have not provided the court with information which would enable the court to determine the attorney's fees. The court does not find that the failure to disclose the documents violated Marsy's Law. Marsy's Law does not refer to a public agency's failure to comply with requests made pursuant to the California Public Records Act. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling and comply with CRC 3.1312. The Parties agreed to allow the City and County of San Francisco four weeks to produce documents.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
